Enable Timescale Extension
query: CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS timescaledb;

name: Table Incident
query:
CREATE TABLE incidents (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    fingerprint TEXT UNIQUE,

    alert_name TEXT,
    system TEXT,
    
    status TEXT, -- OPEN / ACK / IN_PROGRESS / RESOLVED

    start_time TIMESTAMP,
    end_time TIMESTAMP,

    ack_time TIMESTAMP,
    resolved_time TIMESTAMP,

    -- duration_seconds INT,
    -- duration_seconds = EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (
    --     '2026-02-24T18:15:30Z'::timestamp - start_time
    -- ))
    
    -- severity TEXT,
    resolution_type TEXT, -- AUTO / MANUAL

    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

name: Table Escalation Level
query:
CREATE TABLE escalation_levels (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    fingerprint TEXT,

    level INT,
    role TEXT,

    user_name TEXT,
    telegram_id TEXT,

    notified_at TIMESTAMP,
    acknowledged_at TIMESTAMP
);

name: Table Incident Logs
query:
CREATE TABLE incident_logs (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    fingerprint TEXT,

    event_type TEXT, -- FIRING / ACK / ESCALATE / RESOLVED
    
    message TEXT,
    -- value_string TEXT,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMP
);

UPDATE incidents
SET ack_time = now(),
    status = 'ACK'
WHERE fingerprint = '{{msg.fingerprint}}'
AND ack_time IS NULL
RETURNING *;

name: Update incident
query:
UPDATE incidents
SET resolved_time = COALESCE(resolved_time, NOW()),
    status = 'RESOLVED',
    resolution_type = COALESCE(resolution_type, 'AUTO'),
    end_time = '{{msg.end_time}}'
WHERE fingerprint = '{{msg.fingerprint}}';

inline keyboard message
set  answer options
Implementing an inline keyboard
info:
An inline keyboard contains buttons that can
send a callback query back to the bot to trigger
any kind of function.

When the command is received the first output
is triggered and a inline keyboard is shown:

The callback query is received by the event node.
It must be answered like shown as follows. There
you can add your code to trigger the desired bot
command. The answer contains the callback query data in
msg.payload.content

name: Edit an inline keyboard
info:
An inline keyboard can be modified using the
'editMessageReplyMarkup' instruction. To be
able to modify an existing message you need
to know the messageId of the message of the
keyboard. A sample flow is provided in the
examples folder and could look like this:

The mesage id needs to be saved in the flow
or global context (via node save messageId).
This is just a demo assuming that there is only
one single chat.

As next the initial keyboard has to be replaced
with a modified one using the api command editMessageReplyMarkup
command as type (via node edit inline keyboard message).

As an alternative to 'editMessageReplyMarkup' you can also use
the api command editMessageText to relace the keyboard
and also the text as given in the function example edit message
text

The switchnode evaluate callback query just handles the
response and hides the keyboard using another api
command deleteMessage.

name: initial inline keyboard message

Implementing an inline_query
info:
Bots can be called from any chat via inline_query
when the bot is set to inline mode in botfather via
/setinline

name: Receiving location
info:
Locations can be send to the chat. The bot
can receive the longitude and latitude

Sending messages to a specified chat
info:
Locations can be send to the chat. The bot
can receive the longitude and latitude

name: send image
Sending photos, videos, ...
info:
# Flow demonstrates sending a photo as buffer.
As few people do not know that image files can also be sent directly from within memory this example flow was created.
The complete description can be found here:
https://core.telegram.org/bots/api#sending-files

## pic1
File is loaded from a local file and sent as buffer.

## pic2
File is downloaded from internet and directly sent as buffer.

Credits:
flow was derived from see also:
https://discourse.nodered.org/t/taking-a-picture-and-sending-it-via-telegram/27905/4
